Hey guys, JB here. These weekend motivations are really about just giving you cool things to think about food for thought. Have a great day and let's do some serious butt kicking on Monday. All right? Okay, I want to talk to you today about the relationship of goals and vision. This is really important because I got to tell you, I'm in the midst of reviewing some really old footage that I shot for the certification program and there was some point that I came up with that was so profound that I really, I can't believe I haven't spoken about this to you guys ever. It really has to do with what happened to goals. Like what went wrong? Like just to understand that before 1960, no one ever spoke about goals. It was not even like the vernacular. Maybe there was a very small pocket of people who had read Thinking for Rich but it was no, in no way the mainstream that people would say I'm a goal oriented person. It was far outside of the mainstream. It wasn't part of the vernacular. What happened was in 1959 or 1960, it was a record, an actual vinyl record released by a guy named Earl Nightingale called The Greatest Secret and it was this incredibly profound recording he made and it basically is booming voice and it actually paved the way for the company Nightingale Cone, which makes all these self-help tapes. But what it was basically was that you know, you talk about like that, you are what you think about and he took some of them to polling and Hill's teachings and he just was very charismatic and it was just a great way of explaining stuff that you all what you think about and then the cornerstone point he made was that there was a study in Yale University that in 1940, the entire graduating class for only 5% of the graduating class of Yale had set goals and those 5% had outperform the all the rest of 95% combined and that was the thesis that started us the greatest secret that you know, you become a goal oriented person to you all what you think about and people like wow, I mean the 5% outperform the rest well as it turned out it was bullshit, it wasn't true, it was what it was like folklore, it never actually happened, there was no study, it was just it wasn't true right but the interesting thing was is that it started this whole you know trend towards goal setting and you know empowered thinking and hurrah, it was a great thing and through the 60s and 70s it became this big explosion of a new way of thinking and it led to the self-development movement in the 80s and so forth Dr. Baylor at NLP right but here's what happened like anything else you know like the power of something and the idea is in the language like you know goal it was this power fuck I'm a goal setter I'm a goal oriented person well what happened was is over the years that has lost its power entirely everyone's a fucking goal setter everyone's a fucking goals on you know if you're gonna get any resume goal oriented success you know achiever it's it's fucking nonsense it basically became a buzzword for mediocrity people set goals they don't take them seriously they hardly ever tried to enforce they don't set goals the right way and the whole idea of being a goal oriented person is like a past what the nothing it doesn't mean it's not it's a matter anymore right the modern way the effective way of really understanding the importance of goals because goals are fucking crucial you have to have goals but the only way your goals have power is if there's a vision for your future that sits atop the goals in other words you're back in the day when someone said a goal was like there's an emotional investment it was almost like your goal was almost like your vision the back end days of early early early night again was almost like a goal was almost like your vision for the future it wasn't like the way we think of goals now is a beginning date and end date there's weekly goals monthly goals the annual right it was very different like it was a big deal and had this emotional visceral connection to go out and achieve what we wanted our goal was to live a certain type of life and that's really more of a vision nowadays we talk about empowered thinking it's really about vision to have a vision for your future that truly makes you jump out of bed in the morning and sees every day and then what happens is that but that vision is far off it's like three to five years out in the future and what happens is if you try to just focus on something so distant it's very difficult to sort of make it real in your life every day so what we do and the way this starts to really make sense is we connect ourselves to our vision the big V up here we connect ourselves to a series of goals so imagine watch this is where you are this is where you are today your vision represents where you want to be three years of five three to five years now right that's your vision so what your goals are all these little signposts stopping off points along the way they're almost like directional mechanisms that tell you am i headed in the right direction or am i headed in the wrong direction in terms of my future
